text: Rome is the capital of Italy and the seat of government.
Rome is in the Lazio region and is known for ancient history.
Milan is known for fashion, design, and business.
Milan hosts major events like Fashion Week.
Naples is famous for pizza and its historic city center.
Neapolitan pizza is protected by traditional standards.
Florence is associated with Renaissance art and architecture.
The Uffizi Gallery is in Florence and houses famous artworks.
Venice is built on canals and is famous for gondolas.
Venice has landmarks like St Mark's Basilica and Rialto Bridge.
Turin is known for the automotive industry and Italian cinema.
Turin hosted the 2006 Winter Olympics and has the Mole Antonelliana.
Bologna is known for rich cuisine and medieval towers.
Tagliatelle al ragù is a traditional dish from Bologna.
Pisa is known for the Leaning Tower located in Piazza dei Miracoli.
The Leaning Tower of Pisa is a freestanding bell tower.
Genoa is a major port city with maritime history.
Genoa has a large historic center and an important aquarium.
Sicily is an island region with Mount Etna, an active volcano.
Palermo is the capital of Sicily and has Arab-Norman architecture.
Sardinia is an island region known for beaches and rugged interior.
Cagliari is the capital of Sardinia and a cultural center.
The Euro is the official currency of Italy.
Italy adopted the Euro in the early 2000s as part of the Eurozone.
